title: GPU Resource Allocation Policy
content: Welcome to the TrueAI Lab GPU Cluster Management guide. Our compute cluster is partitioned to maximize research efficiency and model training throughput. We offer dynamic access to Nvidia A100 (80GB VRAM) and Nvidia H100 (96GB VRAM) resources. Standard researchers can request up to 2 concurrent H100 GPUs or 4 A100 GPUs using our CLI scheduler. For large-scale training runs exceeding 48 hours, researchers must submit a proposal via the GPU Allocation Form on the internal portal at least 3 business days in advance. Priority is given to projects with upcoming conference deadlines or contract milestones. Idle containers containing a running notebook with 0% GPU utilization for more than 4 consecutive hours will be automatically preempted (suspended) by the scheduler to release resources to the queue. Always ensure you checkpoint your model weights every 10 epochs or at 1-hour intervals to prevent data loss due to preemption. If your job is preempted, it will be automatically placed back in the queue and resumed from the latest checkpoint when space becomes available. For emergency overrides or temporary limit increases, contact the devops-gpu channel on Slack.

title: MFA and Password Security Guidelines
content: To secure user credentials and sensitive research data, TrueAI Lab enforces a strict Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) policy. Passwords must be a minimum of 14 characters, containing at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and two special characters. Passwords must be changed every 90 days, and the system prohibits reusing any of the last 5 passwords. MFA must be configured immediately upon account creation using an authenticator app (such as Google Authenticator or Duo Security). SMS-based MFA is strictly prohibited due to SIM-swapping risks. In the event that you lose access to your MFA device, you must contact the IT Helpdesk directly in person or via verified Zoom call to request an MFA reset. IT will generate temporary single-use backup codes (scratch codes) which you should store securely in an offline password manager like Keepass. If you enter an incorrect password or MFA code 5 consecutive times, your account will be locked for 30 minutes. To unlock your account before the lockout period expires, you can use the self-service portal at https://ssp.trueailab.internal/unlock or contact system administration.

title: Dataset Storage and Ceph Access Control
content: All research datasets must be stored in the centralized Ceph distributed file system or the secure MinIO object store. Storing datasets locally on GPU node root partitions is strictly forbidden and subject to automated cleanup scripts that run every night at midnight. The Ceph cluster is mounted on all login nodes under `/data/shared/datasets`. Read-only public datasets (like ImageNet, COCO, and Wikipedia corpus) are maintained by the data management team in `/data/shared/public`. To request storage space for custom datasets, use the `dataset-cli create <dataset_name> --size <gb>` command. Datasets containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) must be encrypted using AES-256 before upload, and keys must be stored in the Vault service. Sharing access credentials or Ceph access keys with external collaborators is a critical security violation. Any external data sharing must go through the Data Transfer Officer (DTO) and requires an signed Data Use Agreement (DUA). For backup schedules, `/data/shared/user` directories are backed up weekly to offsite tape storage, while `/data/shared/scratch` directories are never backed up and have a 30-day retention policy.

title: API Token Management and Limits
content: TrueAI Lab provides secure REST and gRPC APIs for model serving and dataset access. Users can generate API tokens via the Developer Console under Settings > API Keys. API tokens are split into two categories: Read-only tokens and Full-Access admin tokens. Read-only tokens should be used for inference pipelines, while Full-Access tokens are reserved for deployment management. All tokens expire automatically after 180 days and must be rotated manually by the user. The platform enforces a default rate limit of 100 requests per minute (RPM) and 10,000 requests per day (RPD) per user session for standard endpoints. Premium models (like deep reasoning endpoints) have a tighter restriction of 20 RPM. Exceeding these limits will trigger a HTTP 429 Too Many Requests status code. If your application requires a higher rate limit for production scaling, you can submit a Rate Limit Increase request specifying your expected queries-per-second (QPS) and load profile. For security, never hardcode API keys in source repositories. Always load tokens from environment variables or fetch them dynamically from the secret manager at runtime.

title: Model Deployment to Triton Servers
content: TrueAI Lab uses Triton Inference Server for deploying models to production. To deploy a model, you must package it in the standard Triton Model Repository structure. The model repository must contain a `config.pbtxt` file specifying the model framework (e.g., tensorrt_plan,onnx, or pytorch_lib), input/output tensor shapes, and dynamic batching configurations. Models must be uploaded to the `models` bucket in MinIO. Once uploaded, trigger a deployment roll-out using the `triton-deploy --model <model_name> --version <v>` command. The deployment pipeline will spin up a Kubernetes pod running Triton and mount the model files. Triton is configured with dynamic batching enabled by default to optimize GPU concurrency. The max queue delay is set to 50ms to balance latency and throughput. All deployed models are automatically monitored for latency, throughput, and GPU memory usage. If a model instance experiences a 99th percentile response latency exceeding 200ms over a 5-minute window, the Kubernetes autoscaler will provision an additional replica. Conversely, if traffic falls below 5 requests per second for 15 minutes, the deployment will scale down to a minimum of 1 replica.

title: Code Review and Containerization Policy
content: All code running on TrueAI Lab servers must be version-controlled in the internal GitLab repository and undergo a rigorous code review process. Before merging code to the `main` branch, a pull request must receive at least two approvals from senior engineers. The codebase must pass automated linting checks (flake8/black) and maintain a minimum of 80% unit test coverage. Additionally, all model training scripts and pipelines must be containerized using Docker. A custom Dockerfile must be included in the repository root, based on the official TrueAI Lab base image (`registry.trueailab.internal/base-cuda12.2:latest`). Do not run containers as root; always specify a non-privileged user inside the Dockerfile (e.g., `USER researcher`). Images must be built and pushed to the internal container registry via the GitLab CI/CD pipeline. Kubernetes will reject any deployment utilizing container images from unapproved external registries to mitigate supply chain vulnerabilities. When setting up dependencies, always pin package versions in `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` to guarantee reproducible builds across local testing and cluster runs.

title: System Maintenance Schedule and SLA
content: The TrueAI Lab infrastructure team performs scheduled system maintenance every Sunday from 02:00 to 06:00 UTC. During this maintenance window, the compute cluster, Ceph storage cluster, and API gateways may experience intermittent downtime or increased latency. Critical system updates, security patches, hardware repairs, and database migrations are scheduled during this time. System status and maintenance announcements are posted 48 hours in advance on the Status Page at https://status.trueailab.com and broadcasted in the `#system-announcements` Slack channel. TrueAI Lab guarantees a monthly uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) of 99.9% for core model serving APIs, excluding scheduled maintenance. In the event of an unscheduled outage, the engineering team is paged automatically via PagerDuty. For critical production incidents (Severity 1), our response time SLA is 15 minutes. For general support tickets, the IT Helpdesk operates Monday through Friday from 08:00 to 18:00 UTC, with a response time SLA of 4 hours. You can file support tickets by emailing support@trueailab.com or visiting the Helpdesk portal.
